rag.query: CellProtocol Skeleton keypath Explore owner access

chunks.heading: 1. Encoding Rule (All Elements)
chunks.text: Each element is encoded as a single-key object where the key is the element type and the value is that element's payload. Lists of elements are encoded as arrays of these single-key objects.

chunks.heading: 4. Keypath Rules
chunks.text: Many elements use keypath or url. cell:// URLs are resolved through the CellResolver. Relative keypaths are often resolved as cell:///Porthole/<keypath> in the current UI.

chunks.heading: Skeleton Binding Rules
chunks.text: Skeleton authoring must validate every remote binding before preview or commit. Text.keypath and Text.url require matching get operations. Button.keypath with payload requires a matching set operation.

chunks.heading: Skeleton Explore validator
chunks.text: When --require-owner-access is set, the validator checks for a visible owner/entity access affordance so a generated or user-authored skeleton leaves the owner a path back to their own entity, Co-Pilot, or equivalent shell-provided recovery interface.
